metadata.author: Lackadaisical Security 2025 – Linguistic Division
metadata.description: Japanese-English lexicon with morphological and cultural information

こんにちは.cultural_note: Standard greeting used from late morning to late afternoon

usage_examples.japanese: こんにちは、元気ですか？

おはよう.cultural_note: Casual morning greeting, used with friends and family

usage_examples.english: Good morning! Did you sleep well?

おはようございます.cultural_note: Polite morning greeting, used in formal contexts or with strangers

usage_examples.japanese: おはようございます、田中さん。

私.cultural_note: Most common first-person pronoun, used by both men and women

僕.cultural_note: Casual first-person pronoun primarily used by boys and young men

あなた.cultural_note: Second-person pronoun; can sound distant, often avoided in favor of names

usage_examples.japanese: あなたはどこから来ましたか？

です.cultural_note: Polite copula, makes statements formal and respectful

だ.cultural_note: Casual copula, used in informal speech

行く.cultural_note: Basic verb of movement, conjugates as u-verb

来る.cultural_note: Irregular verb, one of the most basic verbs

お疲れ様.cultural_note: Essential workplace expression used when someone finishes work or leaves

よろしく.cultural_note: Versatile expression asking for favorable treatment or consideration

usage_examples.japanese: 初めまして、よろしくお願いします。
usage_examples.english: Nice to meet you, please treat me well.

すみません.cultural_note: Versatile polite expression for apologies and getting attention

usage_examples.japanese: すみません、駅はどこですか？
usage_examples.english: Excuse me, where is the station?

ありがとう.cultural_note: Basic expression of gratitude, casual form

usage_examples.japanese: 手伝ってくれてありがとう。

ありがとうございます.hiragana: ありがとうございます
ありがとうございます.cultural_note: Polite expression of gratitude, appropriate in most contexts

usage_examples.japanese: お忙しい中、ありがとうございます。
usage_examples.english: Thank you for your time despite being busy.

学生.cultural_note: General term for student at any level

先生.cultural_note: Respectful term for teachers, doctors, and experts; used as honorific

友達.cultural_note: Common term for friend, suitable for all ages

usage_examples.japanese: 友達と映画を見ました。
usage_examples.english: I watched a movie with a friend.

家.cultural_note: Basic word for house or home

usage_examples.japanese: 昨日寿司を食べました。

家族.cultural_note: Family unit, very important in Japanese society

usage_examples.japanese: 家族と一緒に住んでいます。
usage_examples.english: I live together with my family.

父.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own father

お父さん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's father or address one's own father

usage_examples.japanese: お父さんはどこですか？

母.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own mother

usage_examples.english: My mother is good at cooking.

お母さん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's mother or address one's own mother

usage_examples.japanese: お母さんはお元気ですか？

兄.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own older brother

usage_examples.english: My older brother is a university student.

お兄さん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's older brother or address a young man

usage_examples.japanese: お兄さんは何歳ですか？
usage_examples.english: How old is your older brother?

姉.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own older sister

usage_examples.english: My older sister is a nurse.

お姉さん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's older sister or address a young woman

usage_examples.japanese: お姉さんはきれいですね。

弟.cultural_note: Way to refer to one's own younger brother

usage_examples.english: My younger brother is still an elementary school student.

妹.cultural_note: Way to refer to one's own younger sister

usage_examples.japanese: 妹はピアノを習っています。
usage_examples.english: My younger sister is learning piano.

祖父.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own grandfather

おじいさん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's grandfather or an elderly man

usage_examples.japanese: おじいさんはお元気ですか？

祖母.cultural_note: Humble way to refer to one's own grandmother

usage_examples.japanese: 祖母は料理の達人でした。
usage_examples.english: My grandmother was a master of cooking.

おばあさん.cultural_note: Polite way to refer to someone's grandmother or an elderly woman

usage_examples.japanese: おばあさんはとても親切です。

一.cultural_note: Basic number, also used in counting and ordering

四.cultural_note: Number four, 'shi' avoided in some contexts as it sounds like 'death'

usage_examples.english: We are a family of four.
I wake up at six o'clock.

七.cultural_note: Number seven, both readings are common

九.cultural_note: Number nine, 'ku' avoided in some contexts as it sounds like 'suffering'

十.cultural_note: Basic number, base of counting system

usage_examples.english: I have been living here for ten years.

万.cultural_note: Ten thousand, important unit in Japanese counting system

赤.cultural_note: Color red, associated with celebration and luck

青.cultural_note: Color blue/green, can refer to both blue and green colors

緑.cultural_note: Color green, specifically for green distinct from blue

足.cultural_note: Body part - leg/foot, can refer to either depending on context

心.cultural_note: Heart/mind/spirit, important concept in Japanese culture

usage_examples.english: I am walking in the park.

usage_examples.japanese: 椅子に座ってください。
立ち上がってください。

usage_examples.english: I go to bed at twelve o'clock.
I wake up at six o'clock.

時間.cultural_note: Time concept, used for duration and scheduling

usage_examples.japanese: 昼ご飯はどうしますか？
usage_examples.english: What shall we do about lunch?

月.cultural_note: Month or moon, pronunciation depends on context

usage_examples.english: I'm going to Japan next month.

年.cultural_note: Year or age, very common time unit

usage_examples.japanese: 今日はいい天気ですね。

暑い.cultural_note: Hot temperature, used for weather and environment

寒い.cultural_note: Cold temperature, used for weather and environment

usage_examples.japanese: 日本の食べ物が好きです。

米.cultural_note: Uncooked rice, staple food in Japan

ご飯.cultural_note: Cooked rice or meal in general, very important in Japanese culture

usage_examples.english: I eat bread in the morning.

魚.cultural_note: Fish, important protein source in Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 野菜をたくさん食べます。
usage_examples.english: I eat a lot of vegetables.

usage_examples.japanese: りんごを三つください。
バナナは栄養があります。
牛乳をコップ一杯飲みます。
usage_examples.english: I drink one glass of milk.

usage_examples.japanese: コーヒーを飲みませんか？
usage_examples.english: Would you like to drink coffee?

usage_examples.japanese: 紅茶にお砂糖を入れますか？
usage_examples.english: Do you put sugar in your black tea?
I like green tea the most.

usage_examples.japanese: ビールで乾杯しましょう。
寝室は二階にあります。
usage_examples.english: The bedroom is on the second floor.

風呂.cultural_note: Bath, important part of Japanese daily routine

usage_examples.japanese: 庭に花が咲いています。
usage_examples.english: Flowers are blooming in the garden.

学校.cultural_note: School, very important institution in Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 大学で勉強しています。

会社.cultural_note: Company or corporation, central to Japanese work culture

usage_examples.japanese: 病院に行かなければなりません。
usage_examples.english: I have to go to the hospital.

usage_examples.japanese: 銀行でお金を下ろします。
usage_examples.english: I withdraw money at the bank.

usage_examples.japanese: 新しい店を開きました。

駅.cultural_note: Train station, very important in Japanese transportation

電車.cultural_note: Electric train, main transportation in Japan

自転車.cultural_note: Bicycle, very common transportation in Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 自転車で学校に行きます。
usage_examples.english: I go to school by bicycle.
I was scolded by my mother.

usage_examples.japanese: とても楽しい時間でした。
usage_examples.english: It was a very enjoyable time.

つまらない.cultural_note: Expressing boredom or lack of interest

usage_examples.japanese: つまらない映画でした。
新しい車を買いました。

高い.cultural_note: High, tall, or expensive - context dependent

忙しい.cultural_note: Being busy, very common state in Japan

暇.cultural_note: Having free time or being idle

健康.cultural_note: Health, very important concept in Japanese culture

usage_examples.japanese: 健康に気をつけています。
usage_examples.english: I'm taking care of my health.

元気.cultural_note: Being energetic and healthy, common greeting component

勉強.cultural_note: Studying, very important in Japanese culture

usage_examples.japanese: 日本語を勉強しています。
日本語を教えてください。
ピアノを習っています。
いい考えだと思います。
usage_examples.english: I think it's a good idea.

usage_examples.japanese: 日本語が分かりますか？

できる.cultural_note: Being able to do something or possibility

猫.cultural_note: Cat, very popular in Japanese culture

usage_examples.japanese: 猫カフェに行きました。
usage_examples.english: I went to a cat cafe.

usage_examples.japanese: 動物園で象を見ました。
usage_examples.english: I saw elephants at the zoo.
I saw a bear in the mountains.
I was stung by a bee.

花.cultural_note: Flower, very important in Japanese culture

usage_examples.japanese: 桜の花が咲いています。

桜.cultural_note: Cherry blossom, national symbol of Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 桜を見に行きましょう。
usage_examples.english: Let's go see the cherry blossoms.

森.cultural_note: Forest, important in Japanese nature culture

山.cultural_note: Mountain, very important in Japanese geography

海.cultural_note: Sea or ocean, important to island nation Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 湖でボートに乗りました。
usage_examples.english: I rode a boat on the lake.
I can see stars in the night sky.

季節.cultural_note: Season, very important concept in Japan

usage_examples.japanese: どの季節が好きですか？

春.cultural_note: Spring season, time of cherry blossoms

夏.cultural_note: Summer season, hot and humid in Japan

秋.cultural_note: Autumn/fall season, beautiful foliage in Japan

usage_examples.japanese: 秋の紅葉がきれいです。
コンピュータを使います。
ごめんなさい、遅くなりました。

いらっしゃいませ.cultural_note: Standard greeting in shops and restaurants

english: thank you for your hard work
お疲れさまでした.cultural_note: Important workplace expression, shows appreciation for effort

usage_examples.japanese: 今日はお疲れさまでした。
usage_examples.english: Thank you for your hard work today.

usage_examples.japanese: 新しい服を買いました。
白いシャツを着ています。
黒いズボンをはいています。

靴.cultural_note: Remove shoes when entering Japanese homes

usage_examples.japanese: 夏は帽子をかぶります。
usage_examples.english: I wear a hat in summer.

着物.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese garment worn on special occasions

usage_examples.japanese: 結婚式で着物を着ました。
usage_examples.english: I wore a kimono at the wedding.

浴衣.cultural_note: Casual summer kimono worn at festivals

usage_examples.japanese: 祭りで浴衣を着ています。
usage_examples.english: I'm wearing a yukata at the festival.

usage_examples.japanese: コンピューターで仕事をします。

携帯.cultural_note: Short for 携帯電話 (keitai denwa)

usage_examples.japanese: 携帯の番号を教えてください。
usage_examples.english: Please tell me your mobile phone number.

usage_examples.japanese: 新しいスマートフォンを買いました。

usage_examples.english: I read the newspaper every morning.

usage_examples.japanese: ファッション雑誌を買いました。
机の上にコンピューターがあります。
usage_examples.english: There's a computer on the desk.

usage_examples.japanese: 椅子に座ってください。

ベッド.cultural_note: Western-style bed, traditional Japanese use futon

布団.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese bedding laid on tatami

usage_examples.japanese: 冷蔵庫に牛乳があります。
洗濯機で服を洗います。
usage_examples.english: I wash clothes in the washing machine.

usage_examples.japanese: 電子レンジで食べ物を温めます。
usage_examples.english: I heat food in the microwave.
I'll meet my friend at the station.

usage_examples.japanese: 空港で飛行機を待ちます。
usage_examples.english: I wait for the plane at the airport.

usage_examples.japanese: 病院で医者に会います。
usage_examples.english: I'll see the doctor at the hospital.

usage_examples.japanese: 銀行でお金を降ろします。
usage_examples.english: I withdraw money at the bank.

usage_examples.japanese: 郵便局で手紙を送ります。
usage_examples.english: I send letters at the post office.

usage_examples.japanese: この店の食べ物は美味しいです。
usage_examples.english: The food at this store is delicious.

usage_examples.japanese: 友達とレストランで食事をします。
usage_examples.english: I have a meal with friends at a restaurant.

usage_examples.japanese: カフェでコーヒーを飲みます。
usage_examples.english: I drink coffee at a cafe.

usage_examples.japanese: 日本の文化は美しいです。

伝統.cultural_note: Very important concept in Japanese culture

祭り.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese festivals are central to community life

usage_examples.english: I went to the summer festival.

神社.cultural_note: Sacred Shinto places of worship found throughout Japan

神.cultural_note: Shinto deities/spirits that inhabit natural objects and phenomena

usage_examples.japanese: 山の神様にお祈りします。
usage_examples.english: I pray to the mountain god.

usage_examples.japanese: お仏壇でお祈りします。
usage_examples.english: I pray at the Buddhist altar.

天皇.cultural_note: The Japanese Emperor, important cultural and ceremonial figure

侍.cultural_note: Historical warrior class, symbol of Japanese honor and discipline

忍者.cultural_note: Historical covert agents, popular in modern culture worldwide

芸者.cultural_note: Traditional female entertainers skilled in arts and conversation

usage_examples.japanese: 京都で芸者を見ました。

鬼.cultural_note: Supernatural beings in Japanese folklore, often depicted with horns

龍.cultural_note: Eastern dragons are benevolent, associated with water and wisdom

妖怪.cultural_note: Supernatural creatures in Japanese folklore, ranging from mischievous to malevolent

狐.cultural_note: Foxes are messengers of Inari, gain more tails with age and wisdom

天狗.cultural_note: Bird-like supernatural beings, skilled warriors dwelling in mountains

河童.cultural_note: Water creatures with a bowl on their head, mischievous but can be helpful

東京.cultural_note: Capital of Japan, modern metropolis and cultural center

大阪.cultural_note: Major city known for food culture and Kansai dialect

usage_examples.japanese: 大阪の食べ物は美味しいです。

京都.cultural_note: Ancient capital, center of traditional culture and temples

usage_examples.japanese: 京都で神社を見ました。

富士山.cultural_note: Sacred mountain and symbol of Japan, UNESCO World Heritage site

広島.cultural_note: City of peace, memorial site for atomic bomb

沖縄.cultural_note: Southern prefecture with unique Ryukyu culture

usage_examples.japanese: 沖縄の海は美しいです。

北海道.cultural_note: Northernmost prefecture, known for nature and Ainu culture

usage_examples.japanese: 北海道でスキーをします。

奈良.cultural_note: Ancient capital, famous for deer park and Todai-ji temple

茶道.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese art emphasizing harmony, respect, purity, tranquility

書道.cultural_note: Traditional art of beautiful writing, meditation through brush strokes

華道.cultural_note: Traditional art of flower arranging emphasizing natural beauty

武道.cultural_note: Traditional combat arts emphasizing discipline and spiritual development

柔道.cultural_note: Olympic martial art developed in Japan, emphasizes using opponent's force

剣道.cultural_note: Traditional swordsmanship using bamboo swords, emphasizes character development

空手.cultural_note: Martial art emphasizing striking techniques, originated in Okinawa

相撲.cultural_note: National sport of Japan with Shinto ritual elements

桜.cultural_note: Symbol of spring and transient beauty in Japanese culture

花見.cultural_note: Traditional spring activity of viewing and celebrating cherry blossoms

紅葉.cultural_note: Autumn leaf viewing is popular cultural activity

禅.cultural_note: Buddhist philosophy emphasizing meditation and mindfulness

和.cultural_note: Central concept in Japanese culture emphasizing harmony and balance

おもてなし.cultural_note: Japanese concept of selfless, anticipatory service and hospitality

義理.cultural_note: Complex concept of social obligation and moral duty

面子.cultural_note: Concept of maintaining dignity and avoiding shame

平安時代.cultural_note: Classical period (794-1185) of court culture, literature, and Buddhism

鎌倉時代.cultural_note: First shogunate period (1185-1333), rise of samurai class

室町時代.cultural_note: Period (1336-1573) of Ashikaga shogunate and cultural flowering

戦国時代.cultural_note: Period (1467-1615) of constant warfare and political fragmentation

江戸時代.cultural_note: Period (1603-1867) of peace under Tokugawa rule, cultural isolation

大正時代.cultural_note: Brief period (1912-1926) of democratic and cultural liberalization

usage_examples.japanese: 大正時代のデモクラシー

昭和時代.cultural_note: Long period (1926-1989) including WWII and post-war recovery

源氏物語.cultural_note: World's first novel by Murasaki Shikibu, masterpiece of Heian literature

usage_examples.japanese: 源氏物語を読みました。
usage_examples.english: I read The Tale of Genji.

english: The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter
竹取物語.cultural_note: Oldest surviving Japanese narrative, story of Princess Kaguya

usage_examples.english: The princess from The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter

徒然草.cultural_note: Classic essay collection by Yoshida Kenko, Buddhist philosophy

usage_examples.english: The teachings of Essays in Idleness

万葉集.cultural_note: Oldest collection of Japanese poetry, 8th century anthology

english: Collection of Ancient and Modern Japanese Poetry
古今和歌集.cultural_note: First imperial anthology of Japanese poetry, established poetic standards

usage_examples.english: The beauty of the Kokin Wakashu

平家物語.cultural_note: Epic tale of the rise and fall of the Taira clan, Buddhist impermanence theme

usage_examples.english: The tragedy of The Tale of the Heike

太平記.cultural_note: Historical chronicle of 14th century wars and political upheaval

大名.cultural_note: Feudal lords who ruled domains under the shogun system

将軍.cultural_note: Military dictator who ruled Japan, appointed by Emperor

幕府.cultural_note: Military government system that ruled Japan for centuries

武士.cultural_note: Warrior class that dominated Japanese society for centuries

刀.cultural_note: Curved single-edged sword, symbol of samurai class

脇差.cultural_note: Shorter companion sword to katana, part of samurai daisho set

槍.cultural_note: Primary weapon of foot soldiers in medieval Japan

弓.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese asymmetric longbow, used in kyudo

鎧.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese armor made of lacquered plates and silk cords

兜.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese war helmet, often ornately decorated

城.cultural_note: Japanese castles were centers of feudal power and architectural marvels

usage_examples.japanese: 姫路城を見学しました。

天守.cultural_note: Central tower of Japanese castles, symbol of lord's authority

usage_examples.english: The view from the castle keep

合戦.cultural_note: Traditional term for military battles in Japanese history

関ヶ原.cultural_note: Site of decisive 1600 battle that established Tokugawa rule

本能寺.cultural_note: Site where Oda Nobunaga was betrayed and died in 1582

織田信長.cultural_note: Powerful warlord who began unification of Japan, known for ruthlessness

豊臣秀吉.cultural_note: Rose from peasant to unify Japan, known for social mobility restrictions

徳川家康.cultural_note: Founded Tokugawa shogunate, established 250 years of peace

源頼朝.cultural_note: First shogun of Japan, established Kamakura shogunate in 1192

武田信玄.cultural_note: Famous Sengoku daimyo known for cavalry tactics and rivalry with Uesugi

上杉謙信.cultural_note: Sengoku daimyo known as 'Dragon of Echigo', rival of Takeda Shingen

川中島.cultural_note: Site of famous battles between Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin

遺跡.cultural_note: Ancient sites that provide evidence of past civilizations

発掘.cultural_note: Scientific process of uncovering archaeological evidence

土器.cultural_note: Ancient pottery provides insights into past cultures and daily life

縄文時代.cultural_note: Prehistoric period (14,000-300 BCE) known for cord-marked pottery

弥生時代.cultural_note: Period (300 BCE-300 CE) of rice cultivation and bronze/iron tools

古墳時代.cultural_note: Period (300-710 CE) characterized by large burial mounds

古墳.cultural_note: Large burial mounds for elite, keyhole-shaped tumuli

埴輪.cultural_note: Terracotta figurines placed around kofun burial mounds

竪穴住居.cultural_note: Ancient dwelling type with floor below ground level

貝塚.cultural_note: Archaeological sites with accumulated shells and refuse

石器.cultural_note: Prehistoric tools made from knapped or ground stone

青銅器.cultural_note: Bronze Age artifacts indicating technological advancement

鉄器.cultural_note: Iron Age tools that revolutionized agriculture and warfare

銅鐸.cultural_note: Mysterious bronze bells from Yayoi period, possibly ritual objects

天台宗.cultural_note: Major Buddhist sect established by Saicho, based on Mount Hiei

真言宗.cultural_note: Esoteric Buddhist sect founded by Kukai (Kobo Daishi)

浄土宗.cultural_note: Buddhist sect focused on rebirth in Amida Buddha's Pure Land

禅宗.cultural_note: Buddhist sect emphasizing meditation and direct enlightenment

臨済宗.cultural_note: Zen sect using koans and sudden enlightenment methods

曹洞宗.cultural_note: Zen sect emphasizing sitting meditation (zazen) and gradual enlightenment

日蓮宗.cultural_note: Buddhist sect founded by Nichiren, emphasizing Lotus Sutra

法華経.cultural_note: Influential Mahayana Buddhist text central to several Japanese sects

般若心経.cultural_note: Short but profound Buddhist text on emptiness and wisdom

公家.cultural_note: Imperial court aristocracy, refined culture and aesthetics

摂関政治.cultural_note: Political system where Fujiwara clan ruled through regency

藤原氏.cultural_note: Powerful aristocratic family that dominated Heian court politics

usage_examples.english: The glory of the Fujiwara clan

院政.cultural_note: System where retired emperors ruled from behind the scenes

十二単.cultural_note: Elaborate formal court dress worn by aristocratic women

usage_examples.english: The beauty of the twelve-layered dress

蹴鞠.cultural_note: Traditional aristocratic ball game, symbol of refined culture

雅楽.cultural_note: Ancient court music and dance, Japan's oldest musical tradition

舞楽.cultural_note: Traditional court dance performed with gagaku music

香道.cultural_note: Refined art of appreciating incense, one of traditional arts

蒔絵.cultural_note: Decorative technique using gold or silver powder on lacquer

漆器.cultural_note: Traditional Japanese lacquered wooden items, highly refined craft

屏風.cultural_note: Decorative folding screens used to divide space and display art

usage_examples.english: Painting on a gold folding screen

襖.cultural_note: Traditional sliding doors with paper or fabric panels

障子.cultural_note: Translucent paper screens that filter light beautifully

山.cultural_note: Mountains are sacred in Japanese culture, often housing shrines

山脈.cultural_note: Japan's mountainous terrain shaped its culture and isolation

峠.cultural_note: Mountain passes were crucial for travel and trade routes

usage_examples.english: To cross a steep mountain pass

峡谷.cultural_note: Deep valleys carved by rivers through mountains

usage_examples.english: The scenery of a deep gorge

渓谷.cultural_note: River valleys often contain hot springs and temples

usage_examples.english: To walk through a beautiful valley

川.cultural_note: Rivers are central to Japanese settlements and spiritual beliefs

急流.cultural_note: Fast-flowing mountain rivers, dangerous but spectacular

滝.cultural_note: Waterfalls are considered sacred, places of spiritual purification

湖.cultural_note: Lakes often associated with legends and scenic beauty

usage_examples.japanese: 琵琶湖は大きな湖です。
usage_examples.english: Lake Biwa is a large lake.

沼.cultural_note: Wetlands important for wildlife and traditional rice cultivation

池.cultural_note: Ponds in gardens symbolize harmony and tranquility

usage_examples.english: Koi swim in the garden pond

海.cultural_note: Island nation surrounded by sea, central to culture and economy

usage_examples.english: To look at the blue sea

海岸.cultural_note: Coastlines vary from rugged cliffs to sandy beaches

湾.cultural_note: Natural harbors crucial for Japan's maritime development

半島.cultural_note: Peninsulas often contain unique ecosystems and cultures

usage_examples.english: Hot springs of the Izu Peninsula

島.cultural_note: Japan consists of thousands of islands, each with unique character

usage_examples.english: To live on a small island

群島.cultural_note: Japan is an archipelago nation with distinct regional cultures

岬.cultural_note: Coastal promontories often have lighthouses and scenic views

崖.cultural_note: Steep cliffs common in Japan's mountainous coastal regions

usage_examples.english: To see the ocean from a high cliff

平野.cultural_note: Flat areas precious for agriculture in mountainous Japan

盆地.cultural_note: Mountain basins often contain ancient cities and cultures

台地.cultural_note: Elevated flat areas, often sites of early settlements

丘.cultural_note: Hills often crowned with temples, shrines, or castles

火山.cultural_note: Japan sits on the Ring of Fire with many active volcanoes

噴火.cultural_note: Volcanic eruptions shaped Japanese landscape and folklore

溶岩.cultural_note: Volcanic lava creates unique rock formations and landscapes

マグマ.cultural_note: Molten rock beneath Earth's surface, source of volcanic activity
